chunk:
CHUNK:010/021
He fiddled with the sling of his MP5, adjusted it a little, and then glanced at the two Dreamcaster security officers strategically placed at either end of the row of four cabins. The muscle from the kitchen were gone, and I guessed the officer responsible for ship security was also rotating his or her staff through the extra resource-stripping assignment. If nothing else, curious passengers kept both security personnel occupied, and tested their patience as they repeated the same polite but firm message that access to this section of the corridor and the ship were temporarily restricted. \u201cAnyway,\u201d Gaba said, lowering his voice. I doubted the security guards spoke Danish, but humoured Gaba when he beckoned for me to lean closer. \u201cArnet is off limits.\u201d \u201cYes,\u201d I said. \u201cBut Rachael\u2026\u201d \u201cYes?\u201d \u201cHe\u2026\u201d \u201cShe,\u201d I said. \u201cRight.\u201d Gaba took a took a breath to reset. \u201cShe reached out to you, did she not?\u201d \u201cShe suggested Arnet had hit her,\u201d I said, tapping my cheek. \u201cShe had a bruise.\u201d \u201cAnd did she want to press charges?\u201d \u201cYes, but we never got as far as that.\u201d \u201cBut you might,\u201d Gaba said. \u201cYou know, if you talked to her again.\u201d \u201cYou want me to talk to her?\u201d \u201cAap,\u201d Gaba said. \u201cTalk to her about Arnet.\u201d \u201cBut she\u2019s American,\u201d I said. \u201cAnd you said\u2026\u201d \u201cRachael Silk is not American.\u201d Gaba grinned, adding, \u201cNot yet. She\u2019s applied for citizenship but has yet to be approved.\u201d I could feel the pinch of curiosity on my brow, and Gaba grinned when he spotted it. \u201cHow do you know this?\u201d \u201cI have my ways, Constable.\u201d Gaba let the thought hang in the air between us for a moment, and then pointed at the security officer to his right. \u201cShip\u2019s manifest,\u201d he said. \u201cThe captain said I could look at it without a warrant, provided one of his crew kept me under surveillance.\u201d \u201cYou mean supervision,\u201d I said. \u201cDo I?\u201d I thought he did, but then, as Gaba said something about what the Americans don\u2019t know can\u2019t hurt them, I guessed it might be a bit of both. \u201cAnd you want me to talk to Rachael?\u201d \u201cYes,\u201d Gaba said. \u201cKeep it casual, Petra. But get it done before the boys relieve us at five.\u201d \u201cYes,\u201d I said, wondering how I was going to casually read Paniula\u2019s diary, and secretly pump an almost American citizen for information about Sean Arnet, his operation, and what, if anything, it had to do with Dreamcaster passengers discovering jewellery in their pockets. Gaba excused himself to have a chat with the closest of the two security officers, and I took another sip of coffee. Paniula\u2019s diary seemed to grow heavier by the minute as I stared at Rachael\u2019s door, wondering if I could just barge in and get it over with. \u201cGaba said casual,\u201d I whispered, channelling Atii between sips. \u201cSo, keep it casual, P.\u201d \n 8 Casual is not one of my strong points. I\u2019m better at intense and impulsive, and, with Gaba keeping one of the ship\u2019s security officers busy, and a conveniently timed arrival of another group of curious tourists occupying the other, I poured another cup of coffee and carried both to Rachael\u2019s door. I knocked quietly and waited, turning my head to nod at Gaba as he made sure the officer had his back to me. When Rachael opened the door, I smiled, offered her a fresh cup of hot coffee, and stepped inside. Rachael wore a stylish red housecoat that looked very comfortable. With her short hair \u2013 shiny and with good body \u2013 I was convinced she could give me beauty pointers, and maybe even teach Atii a thing or two. Rachael reached for the remote to turn the television down, but I stopped her, thinking that the reality show running in the background might provide some welcome noise to make it more difficult should anyone try to listen in. Even a posh cabin on Dreamcaster suffered from poor sound insulation, and as Arnet\u2019s party had booked middle of the range cabins, I decided to channel a little of Gaba\u2019s paranoia just to be on the safe side. Rachael gestured for me to sit on one of two plush armchairs, and she took the other. We sipped our coffee in silence for a couple of minutes, acclimatizing to each other\u2019s company. Rachael\u2019s gaze drifted back and forth to my MP5, and I adjusted the sling to push the gun around my body and tuck it against my ribs, almost out of sight. \u201cYou brought me coffee,\u201d she said. \u201cI guess you want to talk.\u201d \u201cWe were disturbed earlier,\u201d I said, nodding to my left at the spot where Gaba had stood when he came in. \u201cYou were telling me about Sean Arnet.\u201d \u201cAh, Sean, Sean, Sean,\u201d Rachael said. \u201cEverybody loves Sean. Everyone wants a piece of him. But no one really knows, Sean.\u201d \u201cBut you do,\u201d I said, taking a chance and pressing my finger on my cheek. Rachael had covered the bruise with some foundation, but her cheek was still slightly swollen. \u201cThat\u2019s right, honey,\u201d she said. \u201cI know Sean Arnet real well.\u201d \u201cAnd do you love him?\u201d I said. It was bold, and I think if the urge to read Paniula\u2019s diary wasn\u2019t so strong, I might have taken it slower, and tried to be a bit more casual. But after a short pause, Rachael pointed a long finger with red nails to match her housecoat at me and laughed. \u201cOh, you\u2019re a feisty one, aren\u2019t you, Officer?\u201d \u201cCall me Petra,\u201d I said, and took another sip of coffee.\u201d \u201cPetra\u2026\u201d Rachael tapped her chin with her finger. \u201cAnd your friend? The big fellow with the smooth and shiny head. What\u2019s his name?\u201d \u201cGaba,\u201d I said. \u201cAh\u2026\u201d Rachael smiled and clacked her nail against her teeth. \u201cShort for Gabriel, perhaps?\u201d \u201cNo,\u201d I said. \u201cJust Gaba.\u201d \u201cAnd you call him that, do you?
compressed_context:
AGENT: GABA ADJUSTS MP5-SLING ∧ GLANCES AT 2 SECURITY-OFFICERS AT CABIN-ROW ENDS  
CTX: CORRIDOR-SECTION OBSERVATION: KITCHEN-MUSCLE GONE ∧ SHIP-SECURITY-STAFF ROTATION  
SECURITY-OFFICERS REPEAT POLITE∧FIRM MESSAGE:  
  "ACCESS SECTION-CORRIDOR & SHIP TEMPORARILY RESTRICTED"  
GABA: LOWER-VOICE "ARNET IS OFF LIMITS"  
PETRA: "YES"  
PETRA: "BUT RACHAEL…"  
GABA: "YES?"  
PETRA: "HE…"  
PETRA: "SHE"  
GABA: "RIGHT"  
GABA: "SHE REACHED OUT TO YOU, DID SHE NOT?"  
PETRA: "SHE SUGGESTED ARNET HIT HER" ∧ TAP CHEEK "SHE HAD A BRUISE"  
GABA: "AND DID SHE WANT TO PRESS CHARGES?"  
PETRA: "YES, BUT WE NEVER GOT THAT FAR"  
GABA: "BUT YOU MIGHT IF YOU TALK TO HER AGAIN"  
PETRA: "YOU WANT ME TO TALK TO HER?"  
GABA: "AAP TALK TO HER ABOUT ARNET"  
PETRA: "BUT SHE'S AMERICAN AND YOU SAID…"  
GABA: "RACHAEL SILK IS NOT AMERICAN" ∧ "SHE APPLIED FOR CITIZENSHIP BUT NOT YET APPROVED"  
PETRA: FURROWED-BROW CURIOUS  
GABA: "HOW DO YOU KNOW THIS?"  
GABA: POINTS AT RIGHT SECURITY-OFFICER  
GABA: "SHIP-MANIFEST" ∧ "CAPTAIN ALLOWS VIEW WITHOUT WARRANT PROVIDED CREW SUPERVISES ME"  
PETRA: "YOU MEAN SUPERVISION? DO I?"  
GABA: "WHAT AMERICANS DON'T KNOW CAN'T HURT THEM"  
PETRA: "AND YOU WANT ME TO TALK TO RACHAEL?"  
GABA: "YES KEEP IT CASUAL PETRA"  
GABA: GET IT DONE BEFORE 17:00  
PETRA: INTERNAL-PLAN READ PANIULAS-DIARY ∧ PUMP NEAR-CITIZEN FOR ARNET-INTEL  
GABA: EXCUSES-HIMSELF TO TALK TO NEAREST SECURITY-OFFICER  
PETRA: SIPS COFFEE; WAITS AT RACHAELS-DOOR  
GABA: ENSURES SECURITY-OFFICER FACES AWAY  
PETRA: KNOCKS QUIETLY  
RACHAEL: OPENS DOOR  
PETRA: SMILE ∧ OFFER HOT-COFFEE ∧ ENTER CABIN  
RACHAEL: WEAR RED-HOUSECOAT ∧ HAIR SHORT∧SHINY  
PETRA: OBSERVE BEAUTY-POTENTIAL  
RACHAEL: REACH TV-REMOTE  
PETRA: STOP ACTION ∵ TV-BACKGROUND PROVIDES AMBIENT NOISE  
PETRA: SIT ON ARMCHAIR ∧ RACHAEL SIT ON OTHER  
SILENCE FOR A FEW MINUTES ∧ BOTH SIP COFFEE  
PETRA: NOTE RACHAELS-GAZE FOCUSES ON MP5  
PETRA: ADJUST MP5-SLING OUT-OF-SIGHT  
RACHAEL: "YOU BROUGHT ME COFFEE. I GUESS YOU WANT TO TALK."  
PETRA: "WE WERE DISTURBED EARLIER" ∧ NOD LEFT AT GABA-SPOT  
PETRA: "YOU WERE TELLING ME ABOUT SEAN ARNET"  
RACHAEL: "EVERYBODY LOVES SEAN. EVERYONE WANTS A PIECE OF HIM. BUT NO ONE REALLY KNOWS, SEAN"  
PETRA: TAP CHEEK "BUT YOU DO"  
PETRA: NOTE BRUISE COVERED WITH FOUNDATION ∧ CHEEK SLIGHTLY SWOLLEN  
RACHAEL: "I KNOW SEAN ARNET REAL WELL"  
PETRA: "AND DO YOU LOVE HIM?"  
PAUSE  
RACHAEL: POINT LONG-FINGER WITH RED-NAILS ∧ LAUGH "OH, YOU'RE A FEISTY ONE AREN'T YOU OFFICER?"  
PETRA: "CALL ME PETRA" ∧ SIP COFFEE  
RACHAEL: TAP CHIN "PETRA… AND YOUR FRIEND? THE BIG FELLOW WITH SMOOTH SHINY HEAD. WHAT'S HIS NAME?"  
PETRA: "GABA"  
RACHAEL: CLACK NAIL AGAINST TEETH "SHORT FOR GABRIEL, PERHAPS?"  
PETRA: "NO JUST GABA"  
RACHAEL: "AND YOU CALL HIM THAT, DO YOU?"
